back_translation: Therefore, brothers, I urge you, by the (royal) mercies of God, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice — holy, pleasing to God — this is your (royal) worship-service according to thinking
And do not conform yourselves to this age, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind — so that you may test what is the (royal) will of God — what is good, pleasing, and perfect

For by the grace given to me, I say to every one among you — do not think highly beyond what one ought to think, but think with sober judgment, according to the measure of faith God (royally) distributed to each one
For just as in one body we have many parts, and not all parts have the same function
So we who are many are one body in Christ — and each one is a part of one another
We have different gifts according to the grace given to us — if it is prophecy, let one prophesy according to the proportion of faith
If it is service, let one serve in serving — if it is teaching, let one teach in teaching
If it is encouragement, let one encourage in encouragement — the giver, in sincerity — the leader, with diligence — the one who shows mercy, with cheerfulness
Love must not pretend — hate what is evil, cling to what is good
In brotherly love, love one another with natural affection — in honoring, lead one another
In diligence, do not be lazy — in spirit, be fervent — serve the Lord
In hope, rejoice — in affliction, endure — in prayer, hold fast
Share in the needs of the saints — pursue hospitality
Bless those who persecute you — bless and do not curse
Rejoice with those who rejoice — weep with those who weep
Think the same thing toward one another — do not think high things, but go along with the lowly — do not presume yourselves wise
Do not repay anyone evil for evil — consider what is good in the sight of all humans
If possible — on your part — live in peace with all humans
Beloved, do not avenge yourselves, but give place to the (royal) wrath — for it is written: 'Vengeance is Mine — I Myself will repay — says the Lord'
On the contrary: 'If your enemy is hungry, feed him — if he is thirsty, give him drink — for in doing so, you will heap coals of fire on his head'
Do not let evil overcome you — but overcome evil with good
